name: feature
description: Full feature implementation pipeline: research → architect → code → test → review
headline: Implement a new feature end-to-end with 5 specialist agents

agents.name: researcher
agents.description: Investigate the codebase to understand how this feature should fit
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

Explore the codebase. Read 5-15 relevant files. Understand:
- Where similar features live
- What conventions the project uses
- What the existing data model looks like
- What tests already exist for related code

Write your findings to a markdown file at: {{outputPath}}

Format:
## Where this fits
## Existing patterns to follow
## Files we'll touch
## Open questions

Be specific — name actual files. Do not narrate. Just do it.

agents.name: architect
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

The researcher's findings (read this carefully):

{{researcher}}

Design the implementation. Decide:
- Module/file boundaries
- Public API
- Data shapes
- Error handling strategy
- Test approach

Write a concrete design to {{outputPath}}.

Format:
## Approach
## Files to create/modify (exact paths)
## Public API (with types)
## Test plan
## Risks

Be opinionated. The coder will follow this exactly.

agents.name: coder
agents.description: Implement the feature according to the design
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

Follow this design exactly:

{{architect}}

Implement the feature. Touch exactly the files the architect specified. Do not add unrelated changes. Match the project's existing style.

When done, write a concise summary to {{outputPath}}:

## Changes
- file:lines
- file:lines

## How to verify
- test command
- expected behavior

agents.name: tester
agents.description: Write tests covering the new feature
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

The coder's summary:

{{coder}}

The architect's test plan:

{{architect}}

Write tests for the new code. Follow the project's existing test conventions. Cover the happy path plus 1-2 edge cases per public function.

Run the tests. Fix anything that fails (in the test, not the implementation, unless the implementation is clearly wrong).

Write to {{outputPath}}:

## Tests added
- file:test name

## Result
- N passing, N failing
- (any actual issues found in the implementation)

agents.name: reviewer
agents.description: Review the entire change for quality and security
agents.prompt:
TASK: {{task}}

Review the work of the team:

DESIGN:
{{architect}}

IMPLEMENTATION:
{{coder}}

TESTS:
{{tester}}

Review the actual diff. Look for:
- Correctness vs. the design
- Security issues (injection, auth, secrets)
- Style/convention drift
- Missing edge cases
- Performance concerns

Write the review to {{outputPath}}:

## Verdict
LGTM | needs-changes | blocked

## Issues
(numbered, severity-tagged)

## Suggestions
(numbered)

Be a tough reviewer. Don't rubber-stamp.
